name: Schwartz refined 19-value model
description: The refined motivational continuum of nineteen basic values.
citation: Schwartz, S. H. et al. (2012). Refining the theory of basic individual values. Journal of Personality and Social Psychology, 103(4), 663-688.
licenseNote: Value names and original short paraphrases only; no PVQ-RR instrument items or scoring keys included.

values.name: Self-direction: thought
values.definition: Freedom to cultivate one's own ideas and abilities.

values.name: Self-direction: action
values.definition: Freedom to determine one's own actions.

values.name: Power: dominance
values.definition: Power exercised through control over people.

values.name: Power: resources
values.definition: Power exercised through control of material and social resources.

values.name: Face
values.definition: Maintaining public image and avoiding humiliation.

values.name: Security: societal
values.definition: Safety and stability in wider society.

values.name: Tradition
values.definition: Maintaining and preserving cultural, family, or religious traditions.

values.name: Conformity: rules
values.definition: Compliance with rules, laws, and formal obligations.

values.name: Conformity: interpersonal
values.definition: Avoiding harm or upset to other people.

values.name: Humility
values.definition: Recognizing one's insignificance in the larger scheme.

values.name: Benevolence: dependability
values.definition: Being reliable and trustworthy to close others.

values.name: Benevolence: caring
values.definition: Devotion to the welfare of close others.

values.name: Universalism: concern
values.definition: Commitment to equality, justice, and protection for all people.

values.name: Universalism: tolerance
values.definition: Acceptance and understanding of those who are different.
